Description: Tier1 direct lookup: ORA-04031 (Unable to allocate shared memory)
Notes: severity=CRITICAL; fix_tier=OS + Database; runbook='Unable to Allocate Bytes of Shared Memory'
Actual_Agent_Output:
🔴 ROOT CAUSE: ORA-04031
🕒 TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-05T08:57:12

🛠️ RESOLUTION PLAN:
  • If using the initialization parameter SGA_TARGET, then increase the value of SGA_TARGET or reduce the value of DB_CACHE_SIZE.
  • If the shared pool is out of memory, then either use the DBMS_SHARED_POOL package to pin large packages, or reduce your use of shared memory, or increase the amount of available shared memory by increasing the value of the initialization parameter SHARED_POOL_SIZE.
  • If the large pool is out of memory, then increase the initialization parameter LARGE_POOL_SIZE.
  • If the Streams pool is out of memory, then increase the initialization parameter STREAMS_POOL_SIZE, or increase the capture, or apply the parameter MAX_SGA_SIZE.
  • Chapter 74 74-123.

Description: Tier1 direct lookup: ORA-12541 (TNS: no listener)
Notes: severity=ERROR; fix_tier=Network + Database; runbook='No Listener – TNS No Listener'
Actual_Agent_Output:
🔴 ROOT CAUSE: ORA-12541
🕒 TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-05T08:57:12

  • Ensure that the supplied destination address matches one of the addresses used by the listener - compare the TNSNAMES.ORA entry with the appropriate LISTENER.ORA file (or TNSNAV.ORA if the connection is to go by way of an Interchange).
  • Start the listener on the remote machine..
Remediation_Commands:

  2. lsnrctl start
  3. cat $ORACLE_HOME/network/admin/listener.ora
  4. cat $ORACLE_HOME/network/admin/tnsnames.ora
  5. tnsping <service_name>
  6. iptables -L -n | grep 1521
  7. firewall-cmd --list-all
  8. firewall-cmd --zone=public --add-port=1521/tcp --permanent
  9. firewall-cmd --reload
  10. sqlplus / as sysdba
  11. SQL> SELECT VALUE FROM V$PARAMETER WHERE NAME='service_names';
  12. SQL> ALTER SYSTEM REGISTER;
  13. SQL> EXIT;

Description: Tier1 direct lookup: ORA-12170 (TNS: Connect timeout occurred)
Notes: severity=ERROR; fix_tier=Network; runbook='TNS Connect Timeout Occurred'
Actual_Agent_Output:
🔴 ROOT CAUSE: ORA-12170
🕒 TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-05T08:57:12

🛠️ RESOLUTION PLAN:
  • If the error occurred because of a slow network or system, reconfigure one or all of the parameters SQLNET.INBOUND_CONNECT_TIMEOUT, SQLNET.SEND_TIMEOUT, SQLNET.RECV_TIMEOUT in sqlnet.ora to larger values.
  • If a malicious client is suspected, use the address in sqlnet.log to identify the source and restrict access.
  • Note that logged addresses may not be reliable as they can be forged (e.g.
  • in TCP/IP)..
Remediation_Commands:
1. tnsping <service_name>
  2. ping -c 4 <db_host>
  3. netstat -s | grep -i 'failed connection\|timeout'
  4. ss -antp | grep 1521
  5. cat $ORACLE_HOME/network/admin/sqlnet.ora
  6. sysctl net.nf_conntrack_max
  7. cat /proc/sys/net/netfilter/nf_conntrack_count
  8. iptables -L -n | grep DROP
